reasoning: The authority figure (an older brother) has absolutely no legal or moral standing to seize the property of a competent adult sibling or restrict their movement. A reasonable person would immediately recognize the brother's claim to be a 'patriarch' as having no validity over an adult's titled property.

A partner of only two weeks has no established parental authority to install invasive spyware (keylogging) on a 17-year-old's device, particularly when the biological parent's consent was only for general setup. The overreach is obvious and the authority figure lacks the legitimacy to impose such strict surveillance.
